advantages: Captures non-linear manifold structure that linear PCA cannot unfold—useful when curved clusters appear in embedding space.
Often preserves both local neighborhoods and broader global layout better than classic t-SNE, making multi-branch datasets easier to read.
Scales to larger datasets than naive t-SNE implementations and supports n_components > 2 for downstream use.
Single coherent API (umap-learn) with interpretable knobs n_neighbors and min_dist for tuning visualization goals.
disadvantages: Stochastic and hyperparameter-sensitive—different random_state or n_neighbors values can change plot appearance materially.
Low-dimensional distances are approximate; do not treat 2D inter-point distances as exact high-dimensional measurements.
Less interpretable than PCA loadings—axes are not variance-ranked linear combinations of original features.
Computation still heavier than PCA; very large n or high p may need subsampling, approximate neighbors, or GPU variants.
